revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح هيروس]][[en:Hirose Taisha]][[fr:Hirose-taisha]][[ja:廣瀬大社]][[ru:Хиросэ-тайся]][[sv:Hirose-taisha]][[zh:廣瀨大社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = ''Hirose-taisha''
廣瀬大社 | image = File:Hirose Jinja Haiden.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'', or the prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.59166667|N|135.74833333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{Ublist | ''Wakaukanome-no-mikoto'', | ''Kushitama-no-mikoto'', | and ''Honoikaduchi-no-mikoto'' }} | founded_by = {{ill|Tenmu|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} | established = {{Ublist | 69 {{ill|Before Christ|en|Before Christ|qid=Q92851113|lt=BC|1=Before Christ}} (allegedly) / | 675 }} | date_destroyed = | location = 99 Kawai, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|qid=Q1203880|lt=Kawai-cho|1=Kawai, Nara}}, [[Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi-gun]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = {{URL|http://www.hirosetaisya.com}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = {{Ublist | ''Sunakake-matsuri'' (February 11th), | ''Reisai'' (April 4th), | and ''Õimi-no-matsuri'' (August 21st) }} | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kawai|qid=Q1203880|1=Kawai, Nara}} }} [[File:Second Torii at Hirose Taisha.jpg|thumb|right|The second ''[[torii]]'' at Hirose Shrine.]] [[File:Hirose Shrine Haiden 01.jpg|thumb|right|The [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] at Hirose Shrine.]] [[File:Hirose Shrine Shinme-sha.jpg|thumb|right|Shinme-sha (sacred horse house) at Hirose Shrine.]] {{nihongo|'''Hirose Shrine'''|廣瀬大社|''Hirose-taisha''}}, also referred to as '''''Hirose-jinja''''', is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|qid=Q1203880|lt=Kawai|1=Kawai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.] In 965, {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|qid=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}} ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''kami'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Hirose Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116-117. From 1871 through 1946, Hirose Shrine was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == See also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|qid=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] == Notes == {{reflist|2}} == External links == * {{Official website|http://www.hirosetaisya.com}} {{in lang|ja}} == References == * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/3994492 OCLC 399449] * _______. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] {{coord|34|35|30|N|135|44|54|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[en:Hirose Taisha]] [[arz:ضريح هيروس]] {{Commons category|Hirose-taisha}} [[fr:Hirose-taisha]] [[ja:廣瀬大社]] [[ru:Хиросэ-тайся]] [[sv:Hirose-taisha]] [[zh:廣瀨大社]] {{wikidata link|Q705281}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-zukuri]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
